title: Base 1 Tue — 5 km Easy
note:
Base Week 1 — Rebuilding
3 runs: Tue 5 km easy, Thu 5 km easy, Sun 8 km long.
Pace: 6:30–7:00/km. HR under 140 bpm.
Mon: easy Peloton or walk. Fri: rest.
Focus: consistency and keeping the knee happy.

title: Base 1 Thu — 5 km Easy
note:
Base Week 1 — Rebuilding
3 runs: Tue 5 km easy, Thu 5 km easy, Sun 8 km long.
Pace: 6:30–7:00/km. HR under 140 bpm.
Mon: easy Peloton or walk. Fri: rest.
Focus: consistency and keeping the knee happy.

title: Base 1 Sun — 8 km Long Run
note:
Base Week 1 — Rebuilding
3 runs: Tue 5 km easy, Thu 5 km easy, Sun 8 km long.
Pace: 6:30–7:00/km. HR under 140 bpm.
Mon: easy Peloton or walk. Fri: rest.
Focus: consistency and keeping the knee happy.

title: Base 2 Tue — 6 km Easy
note:
Base Week 2 — Building
4 runs: Tue 6 km easy, Thu 6 km easy, Sat 5 km easy, Sun 10 km long.
Pace: 6:20–6:45/km. HR 130–145 bpm.
Practice carrying fuel on Sunday — test your gel/chew strategy.

title: Base 2 Thu — 6 km Easy
note:
Base Week 2 — Building
4 runs: Tue 6 km easy, Thu 6 km easy, Sat 5 km easy, Sun 10 km long.
Pace: 6:20–6:45/km. HR 130–145 bpm.
Practice carrying fuel on Sunday — test your gel/chew strategy.

title: Base 2 Sun — 10 km Long Run
note:
Base Week 2 — Building
4 runs: Tue 6 km easy, Thu 6 km easy, Sat 5 km easy, Sun 10 km long.
Pace: 6:20–6:45/km. HR 130–145 bpm.
Practice carrying fuel on Sunday — test your gel/chew strategy.

title: Base 3 Tue — 6 km Easy
note:
Base Week 3 — Consolidate
4 runs: Tue 6 km easy, Thu 6 km easy, Sat 6 km easy, Sun 12 km long.
Pace: 6:15–6:40/km. HR 130–148 bpm.
Fuel on Sunday long run. New shoes should be getting broken in now.

note:
Base Week 3 — Consolidate
4 runs: Tue 6 km easy, Thu 6 km easy, Sat 6 km easy, Sun 12 km long.
Pace: 6:15–6:40/km. HR 130–148 bpm.

title: Base 4 Tue — 7 km Easy
note:
Base Week 4 — Final Prep (plan starts Jun 15)
4 runs: Tue 7 km easy, Thu 7 km easy, Sat 6 km easy, Sun 13 km long.
Pace: 6:10–6:35/km. HR 130–148 bpm.
Knee check: must feel solid before the 18-week plan begins Monday June 15.
Formal Hal Higdon Intermediate 2 plan starts NEXT MONDAY.

title: Base 4 Thu — 7 km Easy
note:
Base Week 4 — Final Prep (plan starts Jun 15)
4 runs: Tue 7 km easy, Thu 7 km easy, Sat 6 km easy, Sun 13 km long.
Pace: 6:10–6:35/km. HR 130–148 bpm.

title: Base 4 Sat — 6 km Easy

Base Week 4 — Final Prep (plan starts Jun 15)

Pace: 6:10–6:35/km. HR 130–148 bpm.
Knee check: must feel solid before the 18-week plan begins Monday June 15.
Formal Hal Higdon Intermediate 2 plan starts NEXT MONDAY.

title: Base 4 Sun — 13 km Long Run
note:
Base Week 4 — Final Prep (plan starts Jun 15)
4 runs: Tue 7 km easy, Thu 7 km easy, Sat 6 km easy, Sun 13 km long.
Pace: 6:10–6:35/km. HR 130–148 bpm.
Knee check: must feel solid before the 18-week plan begins Monday June 15.
Formal Hal Higdon Intermediate 2 plan starts NEXT MONDAY.

title: Wk1 Mon — Cross-Train
note:
Cross-training day (45–60 min)
Options: Peloton ride, soccer, swimming, easy walking — no running.
Intensity: Easy to moderate (HR 120–145 bpm). Active recovery, not a hard workout.
Peloton: Zone 2 ride — resist the urge to go hard the day after your long run.
Soccer: Fine but treat as cross-train — don't go all out.
Purpose: Aerobic maintenance without impact stress on the knee.

title: Wk1 Tue — 8.0 km Easy
note:
8.0 km easy run
Pace: 6:15–6:35/km (10:00–10:35/mi)
HR: 130–145 bpm (Z2 — conversational)
Feel: You should be able to hold a full conversation. If breathing hard, slow down.
Purpose: Aerobic base building and active recovery.

title: Wk1 Wed — 8.0 km Easy
note:
8.0 km easy run
Pace: 6:15–6:35/km (10:00–10:35/mi)
HR: 130–145 bpm (Z2 — conversational)
Feel: You should be able to hold a full conversation. If breathing hard, slow down.
Purpose: Aerobic base building and active recovery.

title: Wk1 Thu — 8.0 km Easy
note:
8.0 km easy run
Pace: 6:15–6:35/km (10:00–10:35/mi)
HR: 130–145 bpm (Z2 — conversational)
Feel: You should be able to hold a full conversation. If breathing hard, slow down.
Purpose: Aerobic base building and active recovery.

title: Wk1 Fri — Rest
note:
Rest day — no running, no intense cross-training.
Light stretching, walking, or ATG knee work encouraged:
  - Reverse nordics: 3x8
  - Tibialis raises: 3x20
  - Step-downs: 3x10 each leg
Purpose: Recovery. Fitness adaptations happen on rest days, not run days.

title: Wk1 Sat — 8.0 km Pace Run
note:
8.0 km marathon pace run
Pace: 5:20/km (8:35/mi) — your 3:45 goal pace
HR: 148–158 bpm (Z3 — comfortably hard)
Feel: Controlled effort, breathing elevated but sustainable. Not a race.
Purpose: Train your body to feel and lock in marathon pace.

title: Wk1 Sun — 16.1 km Long Run
note:
16.1 km long run
Pace: 6:20–6:50/km (10:10–11:00/mi) — easy, never race pace
HR: 130–148 bpm (Z2, allow drift to low Z3 late in run)
Feel: Comfortable conversation pace. Slow down if HR exceeds 155 bpm.
Fuelling: First gel/chew at 45 min, then every 40–45 min. Carry water or plan route with fountains.
Knee: Back off immediately if patellar tendinitis flares — 3 easy days beats weeks out injured.
Purpose: Build endurance and time-on-feet. The cornerstone of marathon training.

Purpose: Recovery. Fitness adaptations happen on rest days, not run days.

title: Wk18 Sat — 3.2 km Easy — Taper
note:
3.2 km easy run
Pace: 6:15–6:35/km (10:00–10:35/mi)
HR: 130–145 bpm (Z2 — conversational)
Feel: You should be able to hold a full conversation. If breathing hard, slow down.
Purpose: Aerobic base building and active recovery.
